pattern: With at most a dozen columns, each row becomes a bitmask and every choice of numSelect columns is enumerated to count rows whose mask is a subset of the selection.
hints: Encode each row as a bitmask of the columns where it has ones; an empty row is mask zero.
A row is covered by a chosen column set exactly when its mask has no bits outside that set.
Since n is tiny, iterate over all column subsets with exactly numSelect bits and count rows whose mask AND the subset equals the mask; keep the best.

edge_inputs.description: select two columns to cover three rows
single column covers both rows including an all-zero row
all-zero rows always covered regardless of choice
a row needing two columns is missed when only one is allowed

stress.description: full-size random binary matrix with a mid-range numSelect
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(1, 13)
    n = rng.randrange(1, 13)
    matrix = [[rng.randrange(0, 2) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    numSelect = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    return [matrix, numSelect]
stress.note: 12x12 worst case still enumerates at most 4096 subsets cheaply
